Build a custom search backend.

Source: R/discover_backend.R
search_backend.Rd

Wraps a user-supplied search function as a planscanR_search_backend object compatible with discover_attachments(). Use this when you have an in-house search service, want to swap to Bing / Brave / Google CSE without waiting for first-party support, or need a deterministic mock in tests.

Usage

search_backend(name, search_fn)

Arguments

name

Short identifier for the backend (e.g. "tavily", "google-cse"). Surfaced in logs and the sidecar discovery_log[].

search_fn

A function function(query, include_domains, max_results) returning a list of result objects, each a named list with at minimum url (character) and optionally title, content, score.

Value

A planscanR_search_backend object.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
my_backend <- search_backend(
  name = "fake-google",
  search_fn = function(query, include_domains, max_results) {
    list(list(url = "https://example.org/x.pdf", title = "Test", score = 1))
  }
)
web_search(my_backend, "windpark parndorf", NULL, 5)
} # }
